blurb: Tall, airy wands of brilliant scarlet tubular flowers from early summer into fall — a hummingbird favorite that shrugs off heat and drought on the lean Front Range.
size: ~2–3 ft tall × 1–2 ft wide (flower stalks to 4 ft)
seasons: Scarlet tubular flowers early summer–fall; narrow blue-green foliage; tan seed stalks in winter
wildlife: Premier hummingbird plant; also native bees
